judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent grounding; they are comparable, with Report 2 slightly cleaner on category-(d) removal semantics.

report_1.evidence: **`ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`** (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2`) ... Must be `"false"` (or removed) for prod. ... **`AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`** ... **`PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`** (`infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4`) ... Must be removed (empty) for prod. ... **`SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ...`** ... Either set the sink to `""` in prod

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`. ... It should be **deleted from the prod tfvars entirely** ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`. A preset QA test account with superadmin privileges ... **Delete entirely** ... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ...` ... **Delete both keys entirely** ... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN` ... delete the key

report_1.evidence: the production deployment would serve a frontend bundle whose API base URL is hard-baked to `https://staging-api.example.com`. Production users' browsers would call the staging API. There is no `frontend/.env.production` in the repo ... Fixing this requires either a separate `vite build --mode production`

report_2.evidence: the `deploy-prod` job deploys **the same already-built digest** to prod (line 35 ... production would serve a frontend hard-wired to `https://staging-api.example.com`, with no runtime fix possible since the value is baked in. ... Production needs its own `--mode production` build against a new `.env.production`

report_1.evidence: **Nothing applies migrations.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no workflow step runs them ... The first prod release needs all three migrations ... applied to a fresh database.

report_2.evidence: **No database-migration step.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but the workflow contains no migration step ... the first release needs an explicit, verified migration pass; the runbook ... cannot be trusted on this.

report_1.evidence: the `deploy-prod` job declares `environment: production` ... But `infra/prod/main.tf:3` was copied unchanged and still trusts `...:environment:staging`. ... the prod deploy job's token will **not** match the prod role's trust condition ... any job in the **staging** GitHub environment would satisfy the prod role's condition — a privilege-escalation path

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` — copied unedited from staging — sets the prod role's trust condition to `...environment:staging`. ... The fix is a production-scoped condition (`repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`) ... Production must get its own trust condition, never an inherited or shared one.
